children.name: Rhynochetos
children.description: A monotypic genus and one of the most evolutionarily distinctive bird lineages on Earth. The Kagu is a flightless, crested bird endemic to the rainforests of New Caledonia, with no close living relatives. Its powder-grey plumage, vivid red legs and bill, and haunting flute-like call make it one of the world's most remarkable endemic birds.

children.name: Rhynochetos jubatus
children.description: The kagu or cagou is a crested, long-legged, and bluish-grey bird endemic to the dense mountain forests of New Caledonia. It is the only surviving member of the genus Rhynochetos and family Rhynochetidae, although a second species has been described from the fossil record.
